ques: Locate the latest research about gravitational waves that were uploaded to ArXiv this week and provide a brief summary of one article's main findings.
answer: This week's latest gravitational-wave research on arXiv (submitted 24 Jun 2026) includes "Probing Kinematic Anisotropies in the Stochastic Gravitational Wave Background with the SKA" (arXiv:2606.25845) by Cruz, Malhotra, Tasinato & Zavala. Its main finding: forecasting with optimal estimators/Fisher techniques shows that although the SKA Observatory will greatly improve constraints on anisotropies in the nanohertz stochastic gravitational-wave background, even SKAO combined with Gaia-like astrometry stays below the sensitivity required to detect the kinematic dipole from the Solar System's motion, so new observation/analysis strategies are needed.
